title: Managing process parameters
body:
**Context**
Besides managing spools and available filament, the biggest challenge in my workflows is managing process parameters. With multiple printers, you can use the same spool of filament, but likely not the same process parameters, such as:
* Pressure Advance
* Flow
* Hot-end Temperature
* Z-Offset

These values typically differ from printer to printer and might even vary between a 0.4 nozzle and a 0.6 nozzle.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add a capability to create "Printer Objects" that can hold and manage the above process parameters and can be associated with a spool.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Currently, I work around this by adding "Extra Fields" in the convention `parameter_nozzleDiameter_printername`, e.g., `pa_04_voron`.

**Outlook**
Once a proper slicer integration is available (e.g., Orca is WIP), all relevant parameters can be fetched from Spoolman.

title: Add  a "Total Available" on the filaments page
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I want to know how much of a particular type of filament I have. To find this out I have to copy all the spools I have into a spreadsheet and then do the math

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like to be able to pull up my filaments page and see how much of an individual filament I have in stock

title: Add "Adjust Spool Weight" button to the header
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The most common task a person does when they don't have a printer that supports auto updating the amount remaining on a spool is to weigh and update the spool weight. Right now my only option is to find the spool in the list and open up the spool page or click the very small icon in the row to open the update modal. When you are updating 8 spools from a multi color print this is super annoying. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like there to be a button in the header that is "update spool". It will take you through a workflow where you can select the spool with an inline search, update the spool weight and then either "save" or "save and update more" or cancel. 

When searching for a spool to update in addition to having a text field I can just type in the numbers I'd like the ability to quickly filter by color family, manufacturer and/or material. This would allow me to drill to what I am searching for much faster with a mobile device. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
For those that go through the trouble of using QR code labels it would be nice if when you scan a spool it's not just the information but you can have the option to scan and update weight.

title: Automatically Calculate Remaining Weight when entering a spool
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I have a bunch of partial spools I'd like to enter into Spoolman. Sometimes Spools skip getting put into Spoolman before I have a chance to enter them in. Right now I have to weigh the spool, look up the empty spool weight of the manufacturer, manually remove the weight of the spool from my scale weight then put that weight into spoolman. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I want to be able to enter in a "gross weight" or something like that and have spoolman calculate out the remaining filament and used filament values based on the filament I've entered.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I've thought about trying to make a calculated field in the custom section but it doesn't appear to support that. I've thought about adding a "gross weight" field but then I am still stuck doing the calculations manually. 

**Additional context**
Gross weight could also power another feature I am about to enter. :D

title: Display HEX value on Filament Details page
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When I am painting in Bambu Studio I'd like to be able to use the colors I already have in my library. I need an easy way to copy the hex code 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A column with the hexcode of the color value that can be turned on or off and to display it as an attribute on the details page. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I use a color picker app to do this now but it adds extra steps I am not fond of

title: Reconsider adding SpoolType Model
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
while [trying to](https://github.com/Donkie/SpoolmanDB/issues/63) add [eSun's eMarble](https://www.esun3d.com/emarble-product/) to SpoolmanDB, i ran into a related problem; i manually created a manufacturer `ERYONE [Cardboard]` to be able to set a spool weight (175g) that differs from what that manufacturer usually has. not ideal from a schema perspective, but it works.

furthermore, i noticed that when i create a spool "from external" like so
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e1c7db4a-52c6-4a53-8e70-c13c0d2c1a6c)

it does show "Cardboard Spool", but that info is apparently "lost" once the spool is created. so it does take over the "empty weight" as an "override", but i have no way of locally setting this "cardboard spool" "attribute" in any way.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
i have read that this project tries hard to not overcomplicate things, but i'm starting to believe that `SpoolType` *is* a required entity.... otherwise its a lot of manual work to always correct those values manually after creating (with the wrong empty weight due to wrong default spool type).

i would suggest using [this](https://www.printables.com/model/464663-empty-spool-weight-catalog) as a start for a database model `SpoolType`; with a `manuf_id`, and the manuf should probably have a `default_spool_type_id`, and each `Filament` should have a `spool_type_id` as well.. this would then be just another .json with an accompanying schema in the SpoolmanDB.. what do you think?

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The only alternative right now is that the user has to manually correct the weight value for any non-default-spooltype-filaments he creates.. ~works, but is tedious.

title: Import and Export Spools From/To CSV/Excel
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
This feature request is related to the lengthy process of manually adding all your spools to spoolman. I have over 60 spools and I do not want to have to add them all manually, but I do already have an Excel sheet that has all my spools in it.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like there to be an import/export option for spools where you can export all the data shown in the spools page to a csv, or import data in the same format from a CSV into Spoolman. This will save a ton of time setting Spoolman up for the first time, and can also act as a backup for your data.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manually entering all the spool data into Spoolman from my Excel spreadsheet.

title: Log API requests to Spoolman
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I'm trying to diagnose an issue in https://github.com/mdziekon/octoprint-spoolman, where the OctoCprint plugin sometimes reports way too much filament usage. Recently, I've printed a set of Gridfinity bins that weighs about 171,7152 grams of filament, but the reported usage was 3 times as much. The filament density is the same in both Spoolman and in the slicer. There are no mentions of incoming API requests to Spoolman, only outgoing requests to filament lists.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Log all incoming changes of filaments to better diagnose issues.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Deduce filament usage by weighing filament spools before and after a print and comparing weights to Spoolman reports. 

**Additional context**
[spoolman.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18051645/spoolman.log)

title: invalid mount config for type "bind"
body:
**Describe the bug**
When composing the docker image, the error of 
```Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type "bind": bind source path does not exist: /home/raspberry/spoolman/data```  is returned to the user

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
Copy the first install code block
```
version: '3.8'
services:
  spoolman:
    image: ghcr.io/donkie/spoolman:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      # Mount the host machine's ./data directory into the container's /home/app/.local/share/spoolman directory
      - type: bind
        source: ./data # This is where the data will be stored locally. Could also be set to for example `source: /home/pi/printer_data/spoolman`.
        target: /home/app/.local/share/spoolman # Do NOT modify this line
    ports:
      # Map the host machine's port 7912 to the container's port 8000
      - "7912:8000"
    environment:
      - TZ=Europe/Stockholm # Optional, defaults to UTC
```
Try to compose docker

**Expected behavior**
Docker image should compose correctly.

The issue is fixed by doing ```mkdir data``` in the folder.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

title: Number of spools (and filaments) shown directly in the overview/table (as a column with a number)
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It's a problem related to keeping track of all the filaments (in a fast and convenient way).

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to see, directly in the overview table, how many rolls of filament I have. Or how many filaments (and rolls) a manufacturer has.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
It's possible to click on 'show' for a filament and then click the button 'show spools', but it feels a bit unnecessary to get the info like this. 
OR (very possible) am I missing something very obvious here..?

**Additional context**
I was a little bit confused (amazed? ≡ƒÿä ) why this isn't in there yet. But wouldn't it be handy if each screen (Filaments and Manufacturers) directly show how many spools and/or filaments you have? Especially with multiple rolls of stuff it would be nice to just have a number of how many things you have of something.

For the manufacturers page show two columns with how many spools and filaments this manufacturer has.
For the filaments page show a column with how many spools you have of a certain filament.

This would give a quick and fast overview of the inventory and I assume the numbers might be quite easy to get and implement (famous last words). Just a colum with a number. Or 0 if you have none ≡ƒÿä 

I tried searching in the issues for the same thing but couldn't find it (please redirect if it's a duplicate), there are some things that are kind of similar but nothing 'simple' like this.

title: Add update git-repo style for Moonraker update service
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Updating Spoolman is a manual process, whereas Moonraker has an update manager service based on git repositories Spoolman is not taking the benefit of it. Making the update process more a hassle than desired.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like to install the Spoolman service via just cloning the repository and be able to add this repository in the update manager of Moonraker.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Cloning the repository and making a script that implements the current installation process, so Moonkraker could know when the repository has been updated and install the latest release. Although, this method will include many "false" updates for any repository change that is not included in a release yet.

title: Page doesn't render after adding an extra field with special characters
body:
**Describe the bug**
Upon adding an Extra Field for Filaments (where I set _units_ as "_┬║C_", which I assume is the source of the problem) the page became blank, every other page loads but _Extra Fields_ ΓåÆ _Filaments_ does not.
I had to resort to manually editing the database, dropping the corresponding row (where "_┬║C_" displays as "`\u00baC`" got it rendering again. 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to _Settings_ ΓåÆ _Extra Fields_ ΓåÆ _Filaments_
2. Click on _+ (Add)_
3. Define type as _Integer Range_ and unit as _┬║C_
4. Save

After that the page for Extra Fields for Filaments shouldn't load anymore.

**Expected behavior**
After the new extra field is stored the page reloads normally.

**Spoolman Host (please complete the following information):**

 - Installed version: 0.20.0

title: Import from External and Internal
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When creating new filaments, I can either go through my list and see if it exists, or import from external, as a starting point.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like the 'import from external' button to change to also include internal filaments. It can be listed separate with a different header, but one button that searches both the external database of filaments and the internal database of filaments that can be used as a template.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Searching the internal library first, before going to the external library. No reason to not have both in the same place.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

title: Spool creation streamlining
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Adding new filaments is a bit tedious. Often times you need to create a manufacturer, filament, and then a spool as 3 separate steps. Creating new permutations of existing filaments (colors) can also be tedious, as you must make the filament first, and create the spool as 2 separate steps.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Being able to create a new spool and filament, or spool, filament, and manufacturer, in one step. This could be implemented with a text box for manufacturer/filament, instead of a locked drop down. The user would start entering the manufacturer and filament, If the combination already exists, then it would auto fill the existing parameters. If the combination (either filament or both) does not exist, then they will be created at the same time as the spool using the given information.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Just doing it the way its implemented now.
Bulk filament creation, being able to add multiple filament options from the same manufacturer quickly, with little parameter variance (name and color).
Being able to make a spool when a new filament is made.

**Additional context**
None

title: Attribute colour to spool instead of filament section
body:
Hello,

I just started using Spoolman and it's great!
But to my mind it would make more sense to attribute the colour of a filament to the spool instead of to the filament. My assumption is that the print parameters are mainly related to the filament type of a certain manufacturer and only in rare cases to the colour of the filament. So to me it would make more sense to define the parameters of the filament like PLA +, PLA matte, PLA silk with no link to the colour and then be able to define the relevant colour if I put a new spool in my inventory. 
Just my 2 cents....

title: Data Entry / Ease of Use Suggestions
body:
Greetings! Here is a list of suggestions I have for ease-of-use and faster data entry:

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No, these are strictly feature suggestions. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
- [ ] There should be quick buttons to choose the type of filament for all major filaments, and then an ΓÇ£otherΓÇ¥ button if itΓÇÖs an unknown filament. These should include:
    - [ ] PLA
    - [ ] PLA Pro
    - [ ] PETG
    - [ ] ASA
    - [ ] ABS
    - [ ] HIPS
    - [ ] PVA
    - [ ] PVB
    - [ ] TPU
    - [ ] TPE
    - [ ] PETG-CF
    - [ ] ABS-CF
    - [ ] PA6
    - [ ] PA12
    - [ ] PA-CF
    - [ ] PAHT-CF
    - [ ] ABS-GF
    - [ ] PETG-CF
    - [ ] PET-CF
- [ ] Clicking the filament type should automatically populate the density (PLA, ABS, PETG, ASA, etc all have known densities), but still allow it to be overwritten if it has a unique density, for example different brands of PLA Pro
- [ ] Allow adding a filament from the spool page - if I add in a spool page, it adds the filament. Why should there be two steps necessary for this flow? 
- [ ] Conversely, when I add a filament, give me a number field, where I can automatically add 0, 1, 2, 3 etc spools to inventory without an extra step. After all, why would I be adding that filament if I donΓÇÖt have it already? This will reduce a lot of extra input
- [ ] Quick buttons to populate spool weights, possibly with a hover tooltip and pictures of the spool types, since there are known weights published online for most of the manufacturers spools. 
- [ ] Quick-select buttons for filament diameter, or better yet, a universal setting, for those of us who EXCLUSIVELY stock one diameterΓÇª we should be able to disable that field and automatically assume 1.75. 
- [ ] Quick selection buttons for 250g/500g/850g/1kg/2kg/5kg, so itΓÇÖs just one click, not a click and typing in
- [ ] Save and Add should save, and then go to a blank ΓÇ£addΓÇ¥ page so you can add more without having to click ΓÇ£AddΓÇ¥ again on the next screen - maybe rename it ΓÇ£Save & Add AnotherΓÇ¥
- [ ] Checkboxes to bulk edit, for example, checkbox 5 filaments, change the location on one and it changes all of them. Or archive a bunch all at onceΓÇª etc. 
- [ ] The ΓÇ£HomeΓÇ¥ screen should show me the current amount of filament in weight, and the current inventory value, as well as the spools and filaments and all that. 
- [ ] Add the ability to import via spreadsheet with a set format - this will save a TON of time on data entry, at least for the initial setup
- [ ] In ΓÇ£FilamentsΓÇ¥ it should show me how many spools I have of it as one of the columns

title: Some feature requests after using for several months
body:
I have been using Spoolman for several months now. Using mostly for logging what's on-hand for design work, gives me a pallet of color and material choices at-a-glance.

Some iIdeas I have ...

1-(un)group spools for a project.  I would like to group spools I intend to use for a project for easy access (I have 157 spools in my spool man database making it cumbersome to find what I need quickly).  Then when I'm done with that project I want to remove that group.  I'm thinking these groups are only a way to view spools so they do not need to remove the spools from the overall spool list only group ones I want to see on a per project basis then remove the group when done.

2-update 'used' from Gcode import.  It would be nice to be able to import a Gcode files only to read how much filament is estimated to be used and then be able to tell Spoolman what filament(s) are to be consumed.  I ask this as a way to handle using my Bambu Lab X1C.  My 2 Prusa printers are using Octoprint with the Spoolman plugin so no issues here on tracking usage.  But the idea to read the Gcode file, or at least a better GUI to manually enter filament used would be overall nice for anyone that has a 3D printer to use this software...at least how I am (design color/material choices on-hand and Bambu Lab tracking).

3-adjustable column widths.  I would like to be able to make the columns wider, especially the comments column.

4-link and unlink attributes between Spoolman pages/categories.  I would like to be able to show an attribute on the Spools page that is actually from the Filaments page.  In this example I want to hex color code but being able to chose any would be perfect.  And of coarse be able to unlink them.

Thank for this great work.  I hope you consider my ideas seriously as I think some of them others would like too.

title: selective search for extra fields
body:
Hi, 
I'm currently working on a [PR for Happy Hare](https://github.com/moggieuk/Happy-Hare/pull/335) and we have some optimization that might be useful for our use case. 

We would be interested in fetching a subset of filaments/spools/... using an extra field.

For now something like this is done : 
```py
# Fetch all spools
reponse = await self.http_client.get(url=f'{self.spoolman.spoolman_url}/v1/spool')
for spool_info in reponse.json():
    spool_id = spool_info['id']
    printer_name = spool_info['extra'].get(MMU_NAME_FIELD, None)
    mmu_gate = int(spool_info['extra'].get(MMU_GATE_FIELD, -1))
    if printer_name and mmu_gate >= 0:
        self.spool_location[spool_id] = (printer_name.strip('"'), mmu_gate)
```

What would be nice would be to be able to get only the spools that have the field `MMU_NAME_FIELD` (in our case) at a certain value like follows : 
```py
# Only fetch spools that have extra field MMU_NAME_FIELD matching our printer name
reponse = await self.http_client.get(url=f'{self.spoolman.spoolman_url}/v1/spool/extras/', 
                                     body={MMU_NAME_FIELD : printer_name})
```
Or a similar approach in order to reduce the size of the received payload when getting the wanted spools. In this case the spools assigned to this printer without having to fetch the entire db.

Tell me if anything similar can be envisioned ?

Thanks in advance for your time

title: Documentation and/or tutorial to explain the process of finding the density of a filament
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Yes, being able to put accurate information into the required field of "Filament Density" in order to use the software. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I'd like there to either be detailed documentation or a link to a resource that can help a user find the density of their filaments within the software next to the "Filament Density" field. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I've seen a few threads that go over some "Best estimate" numbers to use for generic filaments with a disclaimer that it might not be accurate, as well as a user-edited database of filament information. Both alt solutions could use documentation like what I'm requesting. 

**Additional context**
I googled several iterations of "How to find the density of a filament" as well as trying to find a datasheet of my filaments that contained density information, but I couldn't find any reliable results. The only somewhat reliable result I did find is [issue #69](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/issues/69): "Auto-populate density field," full of people with similar issues, but there were no usable solutions to finding the density from that post. 

The lack of relevant results could be because of Google's personalization algorithms that make each person's results different based on their search histories, so it is likely that my google results did not include the information I was looking for. 

People have to be calculating the density of their filaments somehow because this software exists and people use it, but my question is how, and if the answer to that question can be documented and linked to next to the field within the software. 

Edit: I want to clarify that this is asking about how to find the density when the filament you're using does not have that information attached. Obviously if the manufacturer supplies that information, you can find it that way, but if not provide a tool to or calculation in order to find the density.

title: Designate a QR code value for the act of clearing the set spool
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Not really

**Describe the solution you'd like**
I would like an officially designated value for the `web+spoolman:` scheme for clearing the active spool in software that integrates with spoolman (e.g. Moonraker)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
* Using a different scheme (e.g. `moonraker:clear-spool`)
* Using the special value "0" `web+spoolman:sp-0`

**Additional context**
I have a barcode scanner hooked up to ESPHome, that generates API requests to moonraker whenever a spoolman QR is scanned. Since I also need a way to suspend tracking, I am currently using `web+spoolman:clear`, which will cause the esphome to POST to moonraker in a way that causes it to suspend tracking.

title: Read Parameters from Spoolman into Printer
body:
Right now the only communication between Klipper/Mainsail and Spoolman is for getting the spool usage data and checking for Filament compatibility.

Now I would appreciate it, if there would be an possibility to get more data from Spoolman for the printing process.
Examples would be:

- Extruder Temp
- Hotbed Temp
- Chamber Temp
- Extrusion Factor

This values above are filament specific and should be the same on each printer.
Furthermore, there are some values that are printer and filament specific which somebody might also want to store in Spoolman (especially in an single printer setup):

- Retraction
- Pressure Advance

I am now asking if there is a possibility to get the data from the Spoolman API and save it as variables in Klipper (that can be used with macros).
Ideally the variables should be updated on every filament change.

Thanks in advance.

title: [FR] Create Spoolman HomeAssistant Add-On
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Yes and no, I'm migrating my old home network docker setup to a new device running Supervised HomeAssistant, and HomeAssistant doesn't like containers that aren't from HomeAssistant running alongside it. I could just install Spoolman on another device, but seeing there's an integration through HACS got me thinking.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Seems most, if not all homeassistant addons run via docker containers, it's just a matter of setting up an add-on configuration with some files that homeassistant needs to build the add-on correctly. There's some example documentation here: https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/add-ons/

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I could just install Spoolman on a different device, but then I don't have all of my self-hosted services coming from one device.

**Additional context**
I know it's not really the primary scope here, and I'm an "edge case", but I think if you had someone on the team who is familiar with Homeassistant, this would be a much better way to deploy a spoolman integration vs just having a HACS implementation. If I could run spoolman via HomeAssistant Add-On Store and get the same functionality the HACS integration offers, that would be awesome!

title: German time format
body:
Hello
is it possible in German time format?
Day.Month.Year
05.06.2024

Best regards

title: Feature request - Add (SDS) Safety Data Sheet & Technical Data Sheet
body:
I would like the integration of Safety Data Sheet(s) & Technical Data Sheet(s) with links to original source(s).

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A way to easily upload files to a location (eg; to a network share or docker container) and to display this information when looking for it into another window eg; if its a pdf or text file to then open it in a new tab or internal window.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Manually adding pdfs/text/jpgs to network share or the like and linking to those from the spoolman DB.

title: Add a filter to the spool select modal to hide already printed spools
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When Printing labels for a bunch of new spools, I need to sort the list to find the spools I just added, or search for them by id, keeping another window open to cross reference.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be great if a checkbox could be added to the modal to hide already printed spools on the "Select Spools" Dialog, and on the "QR Code Printing" Dialog, mark those spools as their labels having been printed once the print button is pressed.

That way the "Select Spools" Dialog can only contain spools that have not yet had labels printed, and that updating is done automatically.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
N/A

**Additional context**
N/A

title: Move the end-of-spool remainder of filament into another, dedicated spool
body:
First of all, Hi! A huge thanks for creating this, it was exactly what I was looking for!
Second, please forgive me if I am not doing something right, this is my first time posting on github and I really don't know what I am doing :) So I hope I am in the right place

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
What I do when a spool is about to run out, I weld the remainder of the filament onto a dedicated spool I keep for this exact purpose. I then use that multi-coloured tail-end spool for test prints or for things I don't care how they look. Like this: [https://ibb.co/z8Zk9nP](https://ibb.co/z8Zk9nP)
That reduces waste and I get more bang for my buck for the filament. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be great if there was a option to declare a spool as an tail-end-collection-spool (not sure what to call this). Then, it would be great if one could migrate the remainder of a spool to that dedicated tail-end-collection-spool (or any other spool, for that matter).

At the same time, I would appreciate if one could select multiple colours for the filament. 

EDIT: Also, it would be helpful if we could enter remaining or used filament amounts in meters, not just in weights. But that may be a separate feature request :)

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
An alternative, of course, is to do it manually, which I am doing at the moment.

I am probably the only one interested in this, but thanks anyway!

title: Make a Spool Grid Summary in Home page
body:
This is just a suggestion, to be able to see all the spools at once. Perhaps just show the spool image with the color and then a tooltip on every spool indicating the name. This way we could see 50 or 100 spools at a glance without going thru the grid pages.

As I said, this is a suggestion. I find the app very interesting and honestly the only one that I could use to catalog my spools, but a quick glance of the colors would be very appreciated.

title: Quickly update location info from list of spools
body: It would be great if when you clicked on the location column in the list of spools it would bring up a pop up to allow you to select a new location for that spool.   This would speed up the workflow.

title: Allow a way to visualize multi color or color changing filament.
body: I have a number of filaments that either change color due to UV or Heat, and some multi color spools.  It would be great to either have an icon in the list for these types or allow the color spool to show multiple colors.

title: Fuzzy Search
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
no problem

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A fuzzy search option. this way you can search fuzzy on each page. it will look in all fields to find something that matches. maybe stick to search within a category (vendor, filaments or spools) based on the page you are on

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
/

**Additional context**
/

title: Add option to show filament length in meters.
body: Most of the other software that I use measure filament use in meters, and using millimeters also gives long numbers that are hard for my brain to parse. I would love the option to change the units that the filament length (and perhaps weight) displayed in. Here is a mockup I made of what the configuration could look like.

title: Separate list for generic filament types
body: A nice to have would be a separate customizable list of filament types such as ABS, ASA, PETG, PLA to be available as dropdown list in Filament -> Material. Perhaps with predefined diameter and density.

title: Add column to filament list for the total available weight of that filament
body:
When I am planning to print, I would like to know how much filament I have available across all the spools of a particular filament.  This would be a quick way to evaluate my total stock of filament when I have multiple spools.

I would like to see a new column added to the filament list view that is the total of remaining weight of all spools of that filament.  I would expect that this value to be sortable like the rest of the columns.

I can currently probe into each filament one by one and view available spools to get a total.  We could also consider a view of spools sorted by filament, but we wouldn't be able to sort by the total available weight.

title: Ability to sort spools by vendor?
body:
Would it be possible to sort the spools by brand (vendor). I didn't find this option...
Thank you very much

title: unistall script?
body:
hi,

is there any script or guide on how to fully uninstall?

title: Filament custom fields available on spools
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I have fields such as "RAL" and "glitter" on filaments, but those also are applicable on the spools of said filament, of course.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Have the Filament custom fields available / viewable / filterable on the spools view

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I don't know? I think filament information is relevant on a spool as well?

**Additional context**
None.

Thanks!

title: Filament changes triggering Spool changes
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

My client uses websockets to monitor spool changes. The payload contains the spool info, but also filament & manufacturer info.
My program uses those updates to update the slicer's filament configuration.

This works well, but I don't get updates to the spools when the filament or manufacturer info is updated. Therefore I also need to have a websocket for those, and I have to store the spools' data in memory so I can update the right spools when a filament or manufacturer is changed.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be nice if it was possible to subscribe in such a way that I would get updates to all the spools using a filament/manufacturer when that is updated.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Just subscribing to all three data types, add an in memory model and update it there. It makes the program slightly more complicated though.

**Additional context**

title: Filter SPOOLS by Filament properties
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
in SPOOLS view, filter by FILAMENT VENDOR is not possible.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add a filters about Filaments properties.

When we add filaments, we are not allowed to setup the ΓÇ£subtypeΓÇ¥, I mean:
Material: PLA

title: Spools Quantity
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

I have some same spool filaments (10 Sunlu Black PETG, for example) and I cannot set the quantity of a spool when I create it. The current mode is to duplicate the same spool, and itΓÇÖs unfriendly and ΓÇ£dirtyΓÇ¥.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Just to add a field ΓÇ£QuantityΓÇ¥, and maybe ΓÇ£Quantity OpenedΓÇ¥. 
Taking into account that ΓÇ£Quantity openedΓÇ¥, I agree to duplicate the spool in order to setup another ΓÇ£locationΓÇ¥.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
The alternative IΓÇÖm considering is to use the field ΓÇ£Lot NrΓÇ¥ to use as a ΓÇ£counter of spoolsΓÇ¥

Thank you!

title: Link as custom field type
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A link type as a custom field. For example, I've added an ASIN field for filaments so I can find the exact spool on Amazon if I want to order more. A direct link could then be easily constructed from this as "https://www.amazon.com/dp/[ASINnumber]", and you could then use that link to go straight to the Amazon listing for the filament. I'm sure many other marketplaces also support constructing direct links from a base URL and their article numbers.

In the custom field creator you could enter a base URL value and in the UI when you edit the value for the custom field you would enter, in this case, the ASIN number. So...

In the settings page when you edit the custom field:
![image](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/62719293/48d23e20-93c3-4083-8ebd-fbf9fa1551c6)
([AN] stands for article number, but having something more generic might be better, not sure what placeholder should be used)

In the filament create/edit page:
![image](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/62719293/31aba09a-34d6-46e3-8d72-b310ca2ad5f9)

Behind the scenes, the base URL and the article number would then be parsed and the [AN] part of the URL replaced by the actual article number, producing:
![image](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/62719293/a786f422-eb42-432f-a686-c8b3ad4fbe88)

If you wanted to get really fancy, regex might also be implemented for URLs with more complex parsing. Might also be useful to have the link parser be able to pull in dara from other fields by their key, like in my Amazon example, as I already have a field with the ASIN number and the key "asin" I could add a Link field with the base URL "https://www.amazon.com/dp/[asin]" to pull in the value of the custom field with the key "asin"

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
What I'm doing now, which is to simply have the ASIN as a regular text field, then copy that into the search box on Amazon. Works, but could be more streamlined.

**Additional context**
I'm sure people could find many more creative uses for this feature than just this one thing.

title: Prevent duplicate vendors by name
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I accidentally entered the same vendor twice

**Describe the solution you'd like**
When save is pressed for a new vendor take that value and squash case and check to make sure that vendor isn't already in the database also ignoring whitespace and present a warning if already entered.

I want to migrate my data to docker and don┬┤t know how to do it - so maybe this could be helpful to have an articel for to support
or
I want at least delete my current installation - also an articel (just delete the dir ?) could be helpful.

title: Unlock ability to create spool with "higher weight" than theoretically possible
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Currently, when adding new spools, Spoolman always assumes that any new spool is "Filament weight" + "Empty spool weight". In theory, this is fine, but as we know it, every spool can vary slightly in weight - sometimes it's a bit less, sometimes it's a bit more. In my case, it's usually the "more" variant, usually 10, 20, sometimes 50 grams more than advertised.

Dealing with "less" works fine - you simply measure your entire spool, and put that weight into the `Measured weight` field...

...but it's impossible to insert spools with "more" weight - even if you do put the exact measured weight, the frontend will show negative number in the `Used weight` field, but more importantly, won't allow you to create / save that spool, as it doesn't pass the backend validation.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I'd like to be able to create spools with more weight than their theoretical initial weight.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Right now, all I can do is put a note for each such spool, and then manually edit spool's data once I use up some filament.

title: Create slicer filament presets
body:
Since I use spoolman regulary and have almost all information regarding filaments in spoolman, it would be nice if we can create filament presets for i.e. PrusaSlicer (and therefore SuperSlicer and other derivates).

A filament preset is only an `ini` file which would look like this:

```init
# PrusaSlicer Filament Preset for Jayo PLA
# Save this content as a .ini file and import it into PrusaSlicer

[filament:Jayo PLA]
bed_temperature = 50 ; First layer bed temperature
bed_temperature_layer_0 = 60 ; Subsequent layers bed temperature
cooling = 1
disable_fan_first_layers = 1
end_filament_gcode = "; Filament-specific end gcode\n"
extrusion_multiplier = 1.0
fan_always_on = 1
fan_below_layer_time = 60
filament_colour = "#RRGGBB" ; Adjust the color code to match the filament
filament_cost = 0 ; Set the cost per kg of filament
filament_density = 1.24 ; Adjust based on filament manufacturer specifications
filament_diameter = 1.75
filament_notes = "This is a generic preset for Jayo PLA. Adjustments may be necessary."
filament_settings_id = "jayo_pla"
filament_soluble = 0
filament_type = PLA
first_layer_bed_temperature = 60 ; First layer bed temperature
first_layer_temperature = 215 ; First layer extrusion temperature
layer_height = 0.2
max_fan_speed = 100
min_fan_speed = 100
min_print_speed = 10
slowdown_below_layer_time = 30
temperature = 210 ; Extrusion temperature
start_filament_gcode = "; Filament-specific start gcode\n"
retract_length = 0.8 ; Adjust based on your printer's performance
retract_speed = 35
first_layer_speed = 20

```
All fields which are missing could be done via #283  I guess, am I right? But the core data is already there in spoolman.
If this feature isn't planned I could write a tool which uses the api to create them. 

What do you think?

title: Labelprinter
body: Hello, I would like to print the labels using a label printer. Would it be possible to export them, preferably as a CSV and adapt them if necessary?

title: Move price & weight into spool
body: The manufacturer of the filament I use produces spools of different weights (and prices) for same material . This forces me to create multiple records for one type of filament. Solution: Move the "price" and "weight" fields to the spools section or create possible sizes and prices for the filament provided by the manufacturer.

title: Multiple Spool Count.
body:
**Describe the solution you'd like**
A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

I would ne nice to have a single entry for the same vendor, type of filament and color, and that then can be checked out from the list and used, and when done can be checked back into inventory after being weighed in.

title: Load Cell Actual Measured weight
body:
Would be good to get functionality like [Filament Scale for octoprint](https://plugins.octoprint.org/plugins/filament_scale/) proivides where you use a HX711-based load cell to read out the current weight of the remaining filament which would then use the empty spool weight to give you the actual weight.

https://github.com/techman83/Filament-Scale-Enhanced

title: Make Save/Cancel buttons always visible in data interentry forms
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
When entering or editing a filament/reel/manufacturer, if there are many fields to fill in, the save/cancel buttons remain at the bottom and you must scroll to the bottom to save. I often find myself editing a field at the top but then having to scroll to save.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Leave the ever-useful pusants (Save/Cancel at the bottom and Back/Update at the top) always visible during editing, putting them, like in a floating canvas.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Alternatively, add keyboard shortcuts to save without scrolling down.

**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

title: Mass editing of multiple rows
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Add the ability to select multiple rows and edit them massively, for example if I want to update the spool weight of 20 different filaments, now I have to open one by one and edit them.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add a checkbox on each row so that one or more rows can be selected. Add a "Massive Edit" button that if pressed opens a form in which all row fields are blank. If you value a field, it will be assigned to all previously set rows. fields left blank will remain unchanged.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
This change can be replaced by exporting/importing data to excel (XLSX or CSV), although it remains convenient to do within the program without having to go through Excel (since a person may not have access to Excel for various reasons).

**Additional context**
Nothing to add

title: Add filament grouping
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It's currently difficult to manage multiple spools of the same material.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be useful for the webUI to show filament grouped by type with a total material available stat. e.g. if I had 3 spools of Prusament Galaxy Black, it would be useful to have a group called "Prusament Galaxy Black" and it to note "3kg available"

Under this I would be able to view the individual spools available. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
-

**Additional context**
-

Is it possible to create a tab where you can view the weekly, monthly and annual filament consumption and costs? As statistics,

title: Custom spool color similar to filament color
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No, no problem at all, just visual/aesthetic request. 

**Describe the solution you'd like**
We have the option to define a filament color with a color picker, would be nice to define a spool color as well. Mostly this is because I have a lot of cardboard spools, but I do know that some vendors (Atomic Filaments) have some uniquely colored spools. There are also clear spools.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I played around with some of the SVGs in the `Spoolman/client/src` folder but I am not versed enough in coding to make it something you can select or change.

**Additional context**
I mentioned playing around with some of the SVGs in the `Spoolman/client/src` folder, I'll share that here.
![image](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/40312912/e90dd915-e21d-43bb-81ab-18294d8e340c)
Not very versed in vector artwork either ≡ƒÿà But that is what I'm looking for essentially. I just pulled this into affinity, renamed some layers, and changed a couple things. Mostly I duplicated the spool walls, spaced them 4.5px from eachother, changed the fill color to a cardboard color, and darkened/added a stroke to each curve. Makes the spool look a little more 3 dimensional.

I'm not sure how all of this works in the background, but I imagine it's somewhat possible. Just more work is all.

Thank you for this fantastic tool, it's so amazing having something like this so nicely integrated with moonraker. ≡ƒÿä 

![icon_spool_thickened_cardboard](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/40312912/b80d9aac-3a0e-4f36-9ecd-1cd123ebcfb6)

title: RFID RC-522
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
RFID rc-522 support for per spool quick ID and possibly filament usage tracking ? like on the bambulab AMS ?

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The rc-522 module would need to be connected to the printers raspberry and communicate with the spoolsman instance that is on an other pi as most of us at least myself host spoolman on a proxmox serverΓÇª (this would require a spoolman client that acts as a proxy for the spoolman instanceΓÇª

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
the elephant in the roomΓÇª.QR code existsΓÇªbut the ease of use isΓÇªworstΓÇª


**Additional context**
i would happily contribute, i can also work on a hardware integration for a voron 2.4.

title: New tab file for active or used coils
body:
Hello,
I have an idea for an extension to include an additional tab on the left-hand side titled Spools in Use.
Where you then press and you can see which coils are currently being actively used. This would have the advantage if you have several printers and all of them are operated from a central Raspberry Pi and there you have an overview of the current status of the spools that are being printed.
This way, if you have several printers, you don't have to go through the hassle of going to each one and checking them.

Hello, it would be good if it were possible to edit or rename the storage location so that this is transferred to all spools that are stored there.

Hallo, es w├ñre gut, wenn es m├╢glich w├ñre, den Lagerplatz zu editieren bzw. umbenennen zu k├╢nnen, so dass das gleich bei allen Spulen die dort eingelagert sind, ├╝bertragen wird.

title: Vertical Text orientation When printing a Label/QR Code
body:
I have a Label printer that has dimensions of 40x30mm for the printed output.

When I disable the "Print Label" option the QR Code is nice and large to scan. 
![Example ](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/10223913/f39c160a-9530-4d25-bdb2-9fb7bdee4347)

But when I enable the "Print Label" option the QR Code will be quite small because now the Text is taking over half of the horizontal printing surface while only taking up a small amount of the vertical printing surface.

![image](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/10223913/314aedec-47c4-4022-b94f-6cf726e0deb7)

I think it would be good to have the option to orient the text vertically instead of horizontally to still have a larger QR Code but also the information of the spool.

A Quick Mockup

title: Update from KlipperScreen doesn't restart Spoolman service

Thanks Donkie for Spoolman and that feature ≡ƒÿè

**Describe the bug**
When hitting update Spoolman in KlipperScreen the service doesn't restart.
And the update button remains visible ├⌐ventuellement if no new Spoolman available (0.15 to 0.15 by exemple)

**To Reproduce**
Click the update button, Spoolman service is stopped, Spoolman is downloaded, Spoolman venv folder is deleted, but Spoolman doesn't seem to be reinstalled (venv not created, service unit still exists in /etc/syst├¿me/system), service can't start.

**Expected behavior**
Spoolman updated and service restarted After the update 

**Screenshots**

title: Add postgres schema option
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
It currently is not possible to select the schema in which the tables are created when using postgres database. I am using supabase as the database and as the tables are added to the default schema spoolmans tables are mixed with my own.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
It would be great to have an env variable SPOOLMAN_DB_SCHEMA, so that you can select which schema to use.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
You could use another database within postgres for spoolman, but this isn't possible with supabase.

title: Add authentication to API
body:
As I don't want to run spoolman on the same system that contains my Klipper installation, I am hosting it on my home server. Securing the frontend is done by integrating Authelia, which makes it very easy. Unfortunately there is no way to secure the API.

It would be great if there was an option to require an API key. The most basic implementation could be a unmutable key supplied by an environment variable on the Spoolman server / container. A better but more work intensive way would be an interface to manage API keys.

Although I haven't looked much into the code yet, I would most likely be able to implement the first option myself. Please let me know your thoughts on that regard

body: I know its probably easier to integrate into the other app/platforms that you've already done but I know with the proliferation of Bambu printers this would be appreciated. I have no clue on how all that works but correct me if I'm wrong but since they have their API setup, could Spoolman just pull from that and update the spools in Spoolman? Either way it looks great and appreciate the work.

title: Add import/export CSV or XML file
body:
Adding import/export option for each category of filament, manufacturer, spools e.g. in CSV or XML file for further processing options in MS Excel etc. 
Many manufacturers allow downloading CSV files of their entire production including barcodes which would make it easier to import into Spoolman after editing in an external spreadsheet program.

title: Inventory Management
body:
So, this will be a edge case for most people, but it  would increase the usefullness of bigger installs (like mine)

For the most standard filaments i have a 5-10 spools in stock. Currently I dont add them to spoolman. Why not? because its more trouble to select the right spool in mainsail then it is to add it to spoolman when i swap out the spool.

So like the archive flag we could have a "storage" flag. When moonraker requests the spools it by default only gets "active" spools.
Maybe later we even could extend moonraker to allow for querieng all spools. (But its not that important i think)

With the added functionality of having a list of spools by given filament (detail page) in the next release (you promised @Donkie  :-) one would have a complete inventory management. without increasing the complexity of selecting the right spool in the printer interfaces.

Having the spool count in the FilamentList itself of "active" and "storage" spools would be a great addition.

body:
I have been using OctoPrint's plugin Spools to manage my spools of filament, but I'm now moving over to a Creality K1 and intend to use Spoolman integrated with Klipper.

It is possible to export the Spools database as a CSV, can this be imported into Spoolman to save time having to reinsert all the data?

I have attached a sample CSV.

Hi, 
The default print QC codes dialog  wasn't enough for me, trying to print labels using my brother label forces me to use their software.

The simplest thing was to export the data to a CSV and go from there.
Using the default export functionality of `refinedev` simply dumps the raw table data to a CSV file, this can be customized but it was enough for me.

The only issue I'm seeing is the QR code generation for those who may try to use the CSV, the code generates it as `web+spoolman:s-${spool.id}`  but it's somewhat not known to the user.

title: More Filament Info in Create Spool Dialogue
body:
first and foremost, thank you for all your efforts. This project is awesome!

I have a small feature request though: We add a lot of details to every filament we add. When adding a new spool only the filament name and material get shown in the selection drop down menu:
![filament](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/16877262/d40b8e85-1416-4a4f-b157-1747ed0ebb94)
As you can see, this gets very confusing, if you have multiple colors and spool sizes of the same filament. Of course, instead of the vendors market name of the filament you could add color and spool size to make the name unique and easily identifiable, but this seems a bit redundant.

Just something to think about.

Again, thank you very much for your work!

title: List caching are not refreshed
body:
Sometimes after entering a Filament or Spool the list does not show the added spool/filament. 
Even hard refreshes of the interface dont change that. After a little debuging the API Endpoints return a 304.

But if viewing the Filament/Spool Detail Page its shown as normal.

After a few days (TTL of the cache i guess) the filament/spools appear as normal)

Version: 0.13.1
Database: mariaDB: 10.9.x

If this is hard to debug, an easy solution for me as user would be a refresh button in the list which enforce a reload from the database - as we have on the detail page.

see comments in #49 too.

![Bildschirmfoto zu 2023-10-29 22-34-01](https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/assets/343160/7441d215-21e6-460c-afb0-c195b49fee2f)

title: Right alignment of numbers
body: Texts or numbers appear in the tables. I've always been inclined to keep the column with the numbers right justified. It's easier to read. It's basically the way it is in Excel. If that could be incorporated it would be nice.

title: Non-uniform colors
body: Love your work, this tool is amazing! As I was putting in some of my filaments, I noticed that there isn't a way to visually represent gradient filaments and rainbow filaments to the database and I'd love to see that added at some point in the future. I'm not good with code, but let me know if there's anything I can do to help!

title: Centralized database of Vendors and Filaments
body: I would be really nice to have a centralized database of Vendors and Filaments that users can pull from and submit new entries to in order to streamline database creation. If the Spoolman container can contact a centralized database to access a community built set of Vendors and Filaments, then we could all benefit from each other's contributions. If a Vendor or Filament doesn't exist in the database we can then submit a request to add it so that others can use it. Once said Vendors or Filaments are on our local databases we can still edit them if needed for our own needs (ie price differences or other tweaks). Thoughts? Would this be possible?

title: Reset spool
body:
I'm planning on reusing the spool registered in spoolman instead of creating a new one, since I usually forget to set which spool is loaded (even harder with multi material) I just configured custom gcode for esch filament and that sets the spool.

When I unpack a new spool currently I need to edit the existing one and say that the used weight is 0, and unset first and last used dates easily.

A reset button that sets used to 0, and clears first and last used dates would cover my needs. I'm aware this may not be what's intended but that's the workflow that works for me.

title: Swap/add vendor and manufacturer properties
body:
Imho the property vendor ist misused for the filament. It would be better if that property would be named into `manufacturer` and every spool gets a new `vendor` property. That would be benefitial when checking which manufacturer actually created that filament and which vendor sold the spool or where do I actually bought it.

The name `vendor` in the context of the filament is misleading.

title: Hamburger Menu oddly positioned on mobile (webapp)
body:
The hamburger menu is pretty odd positioned on iOS safari when using the mobile "app". I would recommend to use the header bar to store the hamburger menu in it. IMHO the floating button is not a suitable control for this.

title: Filter by color
body:
Motivation: I need to print a part in dark - which filaments do I have.

So would be nice to filter by color. I think filtering for the exact hex-value would limit the result too much so probably we could provide the color picker to pick the desired color but select a wider range with similar colors ie through the DeltaE algorithm.

Example: [ColorSimilarity](https://play.vuejs.org/#)

title: Allow the QR code to be configured as a URL
body:
Instead of having to open spoolman and scan the QR code. The QR code can link directly to the spool. This would allow users to be able to pull out their phone and immediately link to the spool. 

This would allow for a streamlined workflow. 

In order to not break compatibility with the current implementation this could ideally, be configureable. 

Edit: To add. My camera is inside my printer and is not easily accessed or has a good view to the outside of the printer so unfortunately that is not really an ideal workflow.

title: QR Code scanning add button with a supported data for a db entry embedded in the QR code.
body:
OP Ref: https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/issues/10

I haven't tested if you can already do this with the edit button or not. Could you add an add button to the scan page where you can click add for a QR code that is not in your database? Which it will then fill in all the values for you from whatever the manufacturer sets. Say the producers of the filament wanted to support Spoolman and had premade Spoolman QR codes on their spools for instance that you just bought or a friend/reseller sent you a spool with the Spoolman QR tag on it.

>Can you add another action button on the spool list that sets the spool id via a call to Moonraker?

Moved to: https://github.com/Donkie/Spoolman/issues/72

title: Create a vendor in filament-create mask
body:
Cause a vendor has _only_ a name it would be cool to create a new vendor from the filament edit mask.

Assume you've bought a brand new filament and want to add it: 
* You need to create the vendor fist
* You add the filament
* You add a spool for the filament

This could be simplified when the vendor drop-down in create-filament  has the ability to add the vendor on the fly: ie if the search does not match a given vendor the current search term has a (+) icon to create the vendor or just allow arbitrary vendor-text strings and create it.

Next, I would suggest an optional "new spool" section with where you can optional enter location, lot-nr, comment and the amount of spools. If this optional "create spools" sections was enabled Spoolman can create the _n_ new spools on the fly without having the user to create "the 3 new filament spools bought from the very new vendor".

PS: Sorry that I put this stuff in a ticket instead of a PR but I'm a Vue guy ┬░;┬░

body: It would be nice if I could add a filament, and have it automatically add a spool of it at the same time. Could add a "create with spool" button in addition to the create filament, or maybe a checkbox to create a spool.

body: Use https://ant.design/components/descriptions perhaps

title: Add possibility to add/edit spool length
body:
Thanks for your great work with Spoolman!

It would be nice to add spool length and not only spool weight.

title: Database of Spool Weights by Manufacturer
body:
First off, this is exceptional work and since I have been watching there has been a remarkable explosion of ideas.

I would like to add one more that I think would help the project.

What about a database of spool weights by manufacturer? It would be great if it were a central database that could accept submissions by users (or ideally manufacturers) for empty spool weights. Then Spoolman could update from that website and not have the user need an empty spool to know what the approximate empty spool weight is.

But I can understand having a central database for all users is quite an undertaking so would it be a good idea to have a dedicated local table (populated by the user) that stores spool weights by manufacturer and type? For example:
Sparta3D | Cardboard Spool | 100g
Sparta3D | Clear Plastic Spool | 80g
Fusion Filaments | Plastic Spool | 85g
Fusion Filaments | Refill Spool | 91g
etc...   (these numbers are totally made up, but I do have these empty spools so I could provide these details for future users)

There would be an added benefit of a pre-made list of manufacturers that could populate a drop-down menu when creating new filaments in Spoolman

title: Printer item
body:
Add optional ability to add printers in the database.

Spoolman would then be the one that keeps track of spool assignment.

SW like Moonraker could still keep track of assignment like before, but could optionally instead assign itself to a specific printer ID, and then run filament tracking on the printer endpoint.

title: Mobile Support
body: Is it possible to get a better layout for mobile? I think there are things like responsive sites that will automatically format the layout to fit the screensize. An app would be ideal, but I know that would take a lot of development and a good mobile site can be just as effective.

title: Search
body:
Being able to search (comments/notes/tags) would be nice too. E.g., you add information like objects you printed with a certain spool, then you can go back and search for that object name and it will show all the spools that were tagged with it. So you search Iron Man, and all the spools you used for an iron man print would show. 

side note:
Is there a way for us to tag feature request or will you just take care of it on your end? I think I have seen githubs that would let the user select. Just trying to save a little work for yourself.

title: Ability to have filament profiles into slicers
body:
Before going any further, I'd like to thank you for this project. It's been on my mind for a long time, but never came to fruition.

Having the ability to retrieve the entire Spoolman filament in your slicer could be useful.

Ideally, we could synchronize from the slicer GUI, but this would require extensive development of slicer parts and as most slicers are managed by major brands, it will be a long road.

Another solution would be to download filament profiles and import them into the slicer. This would require a specific export format for each slicer, as it is not standardized.

Perhaps storing additional information such as extrusion multiplier, cooling, custom g-code could be handy (PA, for example).

title: Address idempotency issues with the spool use operation
body:
The `/spool/{spool_id}/use` operation currently isn't idempotent, which could cause issues if requests gets retried etc.

This could be addressed by either allowing the client to send some unique token with every request, and if that token is seen more than once the request is rejected, or by utilizing the ETag/If-Match system, or perhaps some other way.
